name: Dr. Joseph Mercola
description: Dr. Joseph Mercola is a family physician living in north eastern Florida coast and stopped seeing patients in 2007 to focus on his website, mercola.com which has been the most visited natural health site on the web since 2004. He received his medical degree from Chicago College of Osteopathic Medicine and has been in practice 35 years. He specializes in educating the public about how to apply natural solutions and avoid the dangers of pharmaceutical medicine.

contactInfo.address: 125 SW 3rd Place Suite 250 Cape Coral, FL 33991

name: Dr. Susan Blum
description: Being a doctor isn’t my job, it’s my calling. My mission is to offer the hopeless hope, and to help create long-term solutions for your health. Ever since I was in elementary school, I wanted to be a doctor and help people heal. I’ve always been curious and loved solving puzzles. Being a doctor allows me to be a medical detective to help people live and feel great. For nearly two decades I have dedicated myself to my profession and have helped thousands of people treat, heal and prevent chronic diseases. I do this in my private practice, as a best-selling author and as a professor.

contactInfo.address: Blum Center for Health  34 Rye Ridge Plaza  Rye Brook, NY 10573

name: Dr. Jason Fung
description: Dr. Jason Fung, MD, is a Toronto-based nephrologist and a world-leading expert in intermittent fasting and low-carb diets. He has written the bestselling health books: The Obesity Code, The Complete Guide to Fasting, The Diabetes Code, The Longevity Solution, Life in the Fasting Lane and The PCOS Plan. Jason Fung, MD, born in 1973, received his medical training in Los Angeles and Toronto, specializing in nephrology. He established The Fasting Method (TheFastingMethod.com) to offer evidence-backed guidance on weight management and blood sugar control, emphasizing the benefits of low-carb diets and intermittent fasting. It has become increasingly evident that traditional medical approaches are ineffective for many patients. Numerous chronic health issues today stem from dietary habits and obesity, yet treatments predominantly rely on pharmaceuticals and surgical interventions. Without addressing the underlying dietary factors, there is minimal improvement in the conditions. Therefore, resolving dietary issues necessitates dietary interventions. Dr. Fung authored notable works such as The Obesity Code, The Complete Guide to Fasting, and The Diabetes Code. Additionally, he serves as the scientific editor for the Journal of Insulin Resistance and holds the position of managing director at the nonprofit organization Public Health Collaboration (Canada), which strives to disseminate reliable nutritional guidance globally.

contactInfo.address: 2175 Sheppard Avenue East, Suite 304, Toronto Ontario Canada

name: Dr. Paul Saladino
description:
Dr. Paul Saladino, MD, is a double board-certified physician, renowned host of the Fundamental Health podcast, and acclaimed author of The Carnivore Code and The Carnivore Code Cookbook.

Challenging conventional wisdom in the realm of health and nutrition, Dr. Saladino is known for his willingness to question prevailing beliefs, often sparking controversy within the medical community. His focus on optimal health rather than blind adherence to mainstream ideologies sets him apart.

Graduating from the University of Arizona in Tucson and completing his residency at the University of Washington in Seattle, Dr. Saladino furthered his expertise by obtaining board certification as a Physician Nutrition Specialist.

Driven by a profound realization during his medical training, Dr. Saladino acknowledges the shortcomings of the Western medical system in truly improving lives. He laments its failure to address the root causes of chronic illnesses, instead relying on symptom management through medications.

Central to Dr. Saladino's philosophy is the conviction that nutritional choices play a pivotal role in the development and progression of many chronic diseases overlooked by Western medicine. He adamantly asserts that most chronic illnesses are both preventable and reversible through targeted nutritional interventions.

contactInfo.address: Austin, Travis County, Texas, United States

name: Dr. Sandra Cabot
description:
Dr. Cabot serves as the Medical and Executive Director of the International Health Advisory Service, a philanthropic endeavor established in 1982 to extend assistance to individuals globally through telephonic and online platforms.

Graduating with Honors in Medicine and Surgery from the esteemed University of Adelaide in South Australia in 1975, Dr. Cabot's commitment to humanitarian service became apparent during the early 1980s when she dedicated six months as a volunteer physician at Leyman Hospital, the largest missionary medical facility in Northern India.

Beyond her medical practice, Dr. Cabot is also a proficient pilot, utilizing her Beechcraft Baron aircraft to support the noble cause of Angel Flight Charity. Since January 2007, her aircraft has completed nearly 200 flights, providing vital transportation for patients with severe disabilities residing in remote areas.

Dr. Cabot's expertise extends beyond the confines of clinical practice. She has conducted health seminars worldwide and has been sought after to deliver lectures for esteemed health organizations, including The American Liver Foundation and the Annual Hepatitis Symposium. Notably, her seminars often serve as platforms for fundraising endeavors, generating substantial contributions for local charities.

contactInfo.address: Council of the City of Sydney, New South Wales, Australia

name: Dr. Aviva Romm
description:
Aviva Romm, MD, a distinguished member of the Food As Medicine faculty, boasts a multifaceted career spanning nearly three decades. A mother of four grown children, she holds a medical degree from Yale University and specializes in integrative medicine for women and children. Alongside her medical practice, Aviva is a seasoned midwife, herbalist, and accomplished author, having received accolades for her contributions to natural medicine.

Regarded as "the face of natural medicine in the 21st century" by Prevention Magazine, Aviva has tirelessly championed healthcare advocacy, particularly concerning the well-being of women and children. She approaches her work with a fervent commitment to fostering a healthier world, coupling her medical expertise with a profound social and environmental conscience.

Aviva's influence extends beyond clinical practice; she has held esteemed positions such as past president of the American Herbalists Guild and has contributed significantly to the advancement of integrative medicine through her involvement in founding the Yale Integrative Medicine program. With seven published books, including renowned titles like Botanical Medicine for Women’s Health and The Natural Pregnancy Book, Aviva's impact on natural medicine education is globally recognized.

Recognizing the limitations of the current medical paradigm, Aviva advocates for a shift towards holistic healthcare that prioritizes individualized care and emphasizes health promotion over disease management. She believes that many chronic illnesses plaguing society today are preventable and reversible through lifestyle modifications, stress reduction, and minimizing harmful environmental exposures.

Through her practice of "good medicine" and commitment to transformational healing, Aviva Romm stands at the forefront of the healthcare revolution, challenging the over-medicalization of life and advocating for a more compassionate and comprehensive approach to wellness.

contactInfo.address: 27 W Alford Road West Stockbridge, MA 01266

name: Dr. Paul Jaminet
description:
Paul Jaminet, Ph.D., is a notable figure in various fields, demonstrating expertise in biotechnology, health, and entrepreneurship. As the CEO and founder of Angiex Inc, a pioneering biotech startup dedicated to developing a vascular therapy for cancer, he leads groundbreaking efforts in medical innovation.

In addition to his contributions to biotechnology, Paul is the visionary founder and principal of the Perfect Health Retreat, a wellness initiative aimed at promoting optimal health through holistic lifestyle practices. His insights into health and nutrition are further showcased in his acclaimed book, Perfect Health Diet: Regain Health and Lose Weight by Eating the Way You Were Meant to Eat (Scribner, 2013).

Paul's professional journey has been characterized by diverse experiences. Starting as an astrophysicist at the Harvard-Smithsonian Center for Astrophysics, he transitioned into entrepreneurship during the Internet boom, establishing himself as a software entrepreneur. Presently, he offers strategic counsel to entrepreneurial ventures while actively engaging in economic research, reflecting his versatile skill set and intellectual curiosity.

Paul's personal experience in overcoming chronic illness has profoundly shaped his perspectives on aging and disease, informing his holistic approach to health and wellness. For further information on his work and research endeavors, visit pauljaminet.com. For inquiries, Paul can be reached via email at pauljaminet@perfecthealthdiet.com.

name: Alexander Moskal
description: Originally from New Jersey, I grew up riding in the east coast and fell in love with all that snowboarding has to offer. I've been snowboarding since I was 11 years old. You can find me riding everywhere on the mountain from trees to the park. I love having the opportunity to teach riders, watching them develop both their skills and heir love for snowboarding! AASI Level 1 and children's specialist 1 certified.

name: Dr. Maureen Porter PhD
description:
Using a compassionate and direct approach, I help couples and individuals work through problems and reach their goals in a safe environment.

I specialize in relationship issues of all kinds, including premarital counseling, decisions about whether to stay or end a relationship, recovery from affairs, and step-parenting issues common to blended families, among others. Helping couples recognize their strengths, they learn to repair old hurts, rebuild their friendship, and deepen their connection.

I specialize in individuals going through grief or loss, depression, or a difficult transition in life.

A majority of people who have been in therapy report that it helped them. Usually, people credit the quality of the relationship with the therapist as the key reason for their success, followed by the skills and expertise of the therapist and having clear goals. I believe it is critically important to establish a relationship with my clients that inspires both hope and trust. I believe in working collaboratively to develop goals that make sense and can be evaluated regularly during therapy.

name: Dr. David Holte
description:
Dr. Holte joined Healing Motion Physical Therapy in May of 2024.

David grew up in Iowa where he spent most of his time playing soccer. He graduated from Wartburg College after playing 4 years of soccer and majoring in Biology with a minor in Psychology.

He attended the University of Iowa for graduate school and received his Doctor of Physical Therapy in 2016. While in physical therapy school, David also obtained his Certified Strength and Conditioning Specialist (CSCS) certificate through the National Strength and Conditioning Association. Additionally, he coached competitive youth soccer. After graduating from physical therapy school, David found his way to the Pacific Northwest where he met his wife, Kristen. David completed a manual therapy focused residency and passed his Certified Manual Physical Therapy (CMPT) certificate through the North American Institute of Orthopedic Manual Therapy in 2018. David enjoys treating concussions, dizziness, running, orthopedic injuries, and sports injuries/return to sport.

David has a strong passion for strength and conditioning. This passion led him to start and co-own Advanced Training in Performance, which trains youth athletes in Eugene and Springfield. He has also obtained his Level 1 Precision Nutrition certificate to better help his patients find and maintain a healthy life.

Outside of work, David enjoys spending time with his wife, Kristen, and daughter, Emmerson, lifting weights, and anything outdoors including camping, hiking, skiing, and learning how to fly fish.

name: Ariel D. San Pedro
description:
TFW NorCal specializes in getting clients to lose fat, gain lean muscle and feel good about themselves. Unlike big box gym and fitness centers, TFW NorCal aims at providing small group and one on one personal training to meet the unique needs of 35+ year old clients.

We have satisfied clients from Silicon Valley companies such as Apple, Oracle and Cisco as well as nurses, stay at home moms, retirees, firefighters, police officers, busy parents and weekend warriors. I am a 16 year veteran police officer, P.O.S.T. Defensive Tactics Instructor, Firearms Instructor, Taser Instructor, Impact Weapons Instructor, SWAT Operator and Level 1Gracie Survival Tactics Instructor.

Trainer at: TFW NorCal Fitness Dojo

name: Dr. Todd Miller
description: Miller Method Founder Todd Miller, PhD, CSCS*D, TSAC-F, FNSCA Dr. Todd Miller is an internationally known weight loss and fitness expert, and is the Director of the Weight Management and Human Performance Lab at George Washington University in Washington DC.  He is also an associate professor in the Department of Exercise & Nutrition Sciences, where he developed the Master’s degree program in Strength & Conditioning.  Dr. Miller’s research interests have included studies of exercise adherence in commercial health clubs; caloric expenditure during interactive video gaming in school children; obesity treatment in retired NFL players, and most recently, the role of strength training in preventing and treating overweight/obesity in women.  He has won GW’s Excellence in Teaching Award 5 times.
